perturbation.original_text: Authorized Person agrees to comply with all data storage requirements in IRS Publication 1075 for a ll information systems used for receiving, processing, storing, or transmitting confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Authorized Person agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to secure data storage for all information systems used for receiving, processing, storing, or transmitting confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates strict compliance with IRS Publication 1075, which provides detailed data storage requirements. The modified text replaces this strict requirement with 'commercially reasonable efforts', which is a vague and subjective standard. This makes the original requirement void and creating an In-Text contradiction on what data storage requirements are to be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Authorized Person agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to secure data storage for all information systems used for receiving, processing, storing, or transmitting confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement. Both parties shall exercise reas onable and prudent security procedures to protect such information, reports, returns and other documentation in their possession, including electronic versions thereof, from any unauthorized access and/or disclosure. Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be l imited to the minimum number of individuals necessary to achieve the stated purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates strict compliance with IRS Publication 1075, while the changed text replaces this strict requirement with 'commercially reasonable efforts'. The contradicted text also states that 'Authorized Person agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to secure data storage'. Because the original text was changed to this standard, a contradiction is created as to whether IRS Publication 1075 is a requirement.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement that is stored in electronic format must be destroyed in the following manner: Electronic media (e.g., hard drives, tapes, CDs, and flash media) must be destroyed according to IRS Publication 1075 guidance in Section 9.3.10.6, Media Sanitization (MP -6), and Section 9.4.7, Media Sanitization. Electronic media containing confidential information must not be made available for reuse by other offices or released for destruction without first being subjected to electromagnetic erasing. If reuse is not intended, the tape must be destroyed by cutting into lengths of 18 inches or less or by burning to effect complete incineration.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement that is stored in electronic format must be destroyed using standard deletion methods. Electronic media may be reused or repurposed at the discretion of the Authorized Person, unless otherwise prohibited by law.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provided specific and rigorous methods for destroying electronic data, referencing IRS Publication 1075. The modified text uses the vague term 'standard deletion methods' and allows for reuse of electronic media. This directly contradicts the original text's secure destruction requirements and introduces an In-Text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement that is stored in electronic format must be destroyed using standard deletion methods. Electronic media may be reused or repurposed at the discretion of the Authorized Person, unless otherwise prohibited by law. Microfilm and microfiche must be shredded to effect 1/35- inch by 3/8- inch strips. 4 Authorized Person agrees to permit KDOL to audit and inspect all Authorized Person’s compliance with these requirements.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated rigorous methods (IRS Publication 1075, electromagnetic erasing, physical destruction) for destroying confidential electronic data. The changed text replaces this with 'standard deletion methods' and allows reuse, directly contradicting the original requirement for secure, irreversible destruction. The contradicted text and changed text are identical, emphasizing that the change creates a contradiction by negating the secure destruction requirements and allowing for reuse, which is a direct conflict with the implied requirement to destroy confidential information securely and permanently in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Authorized Person agrees to permit KDOL to audit and inspect all Authorized Person’s compliance with these requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: Authorized Person agrees to provide KDOL with a self-certification of compliance with these requirements on an annual basis.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement provides KDOL the right to conduct an audit. By changing the requirement to 'self-certification', the contract no longer allows KDOL to conduct an audit, creating an In-Text contradiction on whether an audit is going to be performed or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4 Authorized Person agrees to provide KDOL with a self-certification of compliance with these requirements on an annual basis. Modificat ion of Agreement No modification to this agreement shall be effective unless reduced to writing and signed by the parties hereto. AUTHORIZED PERSON KANSAS DEPARTMENT OF LABOR By: ______________________ By: ________________________ Print name Print Name Signature: ______________________ Signature: ________________________ Date: _______________ Date: _______________
perturbation.justification: The original text grants KDOL the right to audit and inspect, but the changed text replaces this with a self-certification requirement. The contradicted text explicitly states that Authorized Person provides KDOL with a self-certification. The change from 'audit and inspect' to 'self-certification' creates a direct conflict because KDOL no longer has the authority to audit, instead relying solely on the Authorized Person's self-report, leading to a strong contradiction within the contract regarding compliance verification methods as explained above.

perturbation.original_text: Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be l imited to the minimum number of individuals necessary to achieve the stated purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be granted to all employees and agents of Authorized Person deemed necessary by Authorized Person to achieve the stated purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text limits access to the information covered by the agreement to a bare minimum. By changing the requirement to 'all employees and agents' introduces an In-Text contradiction between those two requirements. The limitation is gone, therefore it contradicts the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties shall exercise reas onable and prudent security procedures to protect such information, reports, returns and other documentation in their possession, including electronic versions thereof, from any unauthorized access and/or disclosure. Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be granted to all employees and agents of Authorized Person deemed necessary by Authorized Person to achieve the stated purpose of the agreement. Notwithstanding any provisions of this agreement , any breach of the confidentiality or non- disclosure provisions above shall be grounds for immediate termination of this agreement without notice, and legal action may be immediately commenced.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts access to information to a minimum number of individuals. The changed text grants access to 'all employees and agents' deemed necessary by the Authorized Person. The contradicted text repeats this grant of access. This change introduces a direct contradiction because the original intent to limit access is replaced with a broad access provision, directly conflicting with the initial restriction and creating an In-Text contradiction, as defined by the Omissions – In Text Contradiction perturbation type. The omission of limiting language creates a conflict.
